excerpt_en: The market opened at three in the morning. The market always opened at three in the morning. The market was a place where constructs were bought and sold and the people who bought them and sold them were not the people who made them. Sally Shears had opened the market eleven years ago. Sally Shears had been the only operator the market had ever known. Sally Shears had been the only operator the market would ever know. The market was Sally. Sally was the market. The market was three in the morning and a single desk in a single room in a single building in a single district in a single city in a single sprawl in a single world in a single matrix. The market was Sally.
excerpt_ko: 시장은 새벽 3시에 문을 열었다. 시장은 늘 새벽 3시에 문을 열었다. 시장은 construct가 사고팔리는 곳이었고, 그것을 사고팔던 사람들은 그것을 만든 사람들이 아니었다. 샐리 시어스는 11년 전에 시장을 열었다. 샐리 시어스는 시장이 알았던 유일한 운영자였다. 샐리 시어스는 시장이 영원히 알 유일한 운영자였다. 시장이 샐리였다. 샐리가 시장이었다. 시장은 새벽 3시와, 단일 도시에, 단일 스프롤의, 단일 세계의, 단일 매트릭스의, 단일 빌딩의, 단일 구역의, 단일 방의, 단일 책상이었다. 시장이 샐리였다.

_play_notes.narrative_role: Sally is the 7th character — a cold A.I. market operator from Count Zero. Phase 8 adds her as the antagonist POV. Her 4 missions trace her arc: 1) Market opens, 2) Bobby's betrayal, 3) Marly meeting, 4) Angie threat.
_play_notes.ending: Her ending is the market — whether she controls it, sells it, or dies in it.
_play_notes.tone: Sharp, calculating. Short sentences. Every word is a transaction.
_play_notes.replaces_heretic: Opposite of Kas: Kas is an artist who wants to destroy the family, Sally is a merchant who wants to own the market.
